name: Smith & Wesson M&P15 Sport II
description: One of the best-selling AR-15 platform rifles, offering a reliable and affordable entry point for sport shooting and home defense. Features a 16-inch barrel with a 1:9 twist rate.

name: Ruger AR-556
description: A popular AR-15 variant with a cold hammer-forged barrel and Ruger's trademark reliability. Widely regarded as one of the best value AR-platform rifles on the market.

name: Springfield Armory SAINT
description: Springfield's entry into the AR-15 market featuring a Bravo Company trigger guard, mid-length gas system, and Melonite-finished barrel. A strong competitor in the mid-range AR segment.

name: Ruger American Rifle
description: A lightweight, accurate bolt-action rifle with Ruger's Marksman Adjustable Trigger. Consistently one of the top-selling bolt-action rifles due to its quality-to-price ratio.

name: Remington 700 SPS
description: The iconic Remington 700 action has been the foundation for countless precision builds. The SPS model offers an accessible entry to this legendary platform.

name: Savage Arms Axis II XP
description: A budget-friendly bolt-action rifle that ships with a mounted and bore-sighted Bushnell scope. Features Savage's user-adjustable AccuTrigger for a crisp pull.

name: Glock G19 Gen5
description: The quintessential compact 9mm pistol used by law enforcement and civilians worldwide. The Gen5 features a flared mag well, nDLC finish, and Marksman barrel.

name: Sig Sauer P365
description: A micro-compact 9mm that revolutionized the concealed carry market with its 10+1 capacity in an ultra-slim frame. Winner of multiple industry awards since its 2018 launch.

name: Springfield Armory Hellcat
description: A micro-compact 9mm competing directly with the P365, offering an 11+1 capacity and an adaptive grip texture. Features a U-Dot sight system for fast target acquisition.

name: Smith & Wesson 686 Plus
description: A 7-shot stainless steel L-frame revolver that also fires .38 Special. Long considered the gold standard for .357 Magnum revolvers in both sport shooting and defense.

name: Ruger GP100
description: Built on Ruger's notoriously overbuilt frame, the GP100 is known for absorbing heavy magnum loads with ease. A favorite among revolver enthusiasts for its durability.

name: Smith & Wesson Model 642
description: A lightweight, hammerless J-frame snub-nose revolver designed for concealed carry. Its enclosed hammer prevents snagging on clothing during a draw.

name: Mossberg 500
description: One of the most popular pump-action shotguns ever produced, with over 12 million sold. Known for its ambidextrous tang safety and wide aftermarket support.

name: Remington 870 Express
description: The best-selling pump shotgun in history with over 11 million units produced. A workhorse platform used for hunting, sport, home defense, and law enforcement.

name: Benelli M4
description: A gas-operated semi-automatic shotgun adopted by the U.S. Marine Corps as the M1014. Renowned for extreme reliability and the ability to cycle any load without adjustment.
